mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates a broad-based demand contraction, which forces equity prices lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns remain positive despite concurrent declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during economic recoveries.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns turn negative or flat when INDPRO rises during post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth signals robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when GDPC1 increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which immediately suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0 during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates confirms a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns dominate when both series deteriorate.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts indicate renewed confidence in the construction sector and broader economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain negative or flat when HOUST rises during post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.
